browserSearchPath: CLMS Bio-geophysical Parameters, Vegetation, Vegetation Phenology and Productivity Parameters.
collectionDescription: Describes the seasonal growth patterns of vegetated land surface. Provides 13 phenology metrics for up to two growing seasons per year.  Annual estimates are available globally at a spatial resolution of about 300 m, from 2014 to the present. More information here.

layers.shortDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the season start and end.
layers.longDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the start and end of the first growing season. Expressed in [m².day/m²].

layers.shortDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the season start and end.
layers.longDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the start and end of the second growing season. Expressed in [m².day/m²].

layers.shortDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the season start and end minus their base level value.
layers.longDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the start and end of the first growing season minus their base level value. Expressed in [m².day/m²].

layers.shortDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the season start and end minus their base level value.
layers.longDescription: Growing season integral computed as the time-integrated Plant Phenology Index values between the dates of the start and end of the second growing season minus their base level value. Expressed in [m².day/m²].

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year when the vegetation growing season starts in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year when the first vegetation growing season starts in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index. A negative value of the SOSD indicates that the peak of growing season is in the current year and the start of the season is located in the previous year.

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year when the vegetation growing season starts in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year when the second vegetation growing season starts in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index. A negative value of the SOSD indicates that the peak of growing season is in the current year and the start of the season is located in the previous year.

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year when the vegetation growing season ends in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year when the first vegetation growing season ends in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index. A value of the EOSD larger than 365 indicates that the peak of growing season is in the current year and the end of the season is located in the next year.

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year when the vegetation growing season ends in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year when the second vegetation growing season ends in the time profile of the Plant Phenology Index. A value of the EOSD larger than 365 indicates that the peak of growing season is in the current year and the end of the season is located in the next year.

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year in the vegetation growing season when the Plant Phenology Index reaches its maximum value.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year in the first vegetation growing season when the Plant Phenology Index reaches its maximum value.

layers.shortDescription: Day of the year in the vegetation growing season when the Plant Phenology Index reaches its maximum value.
layers.longDescription: Day of the year in the second vegetation growing season when the Plant Phenology Index reaches its maximum value.

layers.shortDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the start of the vegetation growing season.
layers.longDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the start of the first vegetation growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the start of the vegetation growing season.
layers.longDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the start of the second vegetation growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the end of the vegetation growing season.
layers.longDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the end of the first vegetation growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the end of the vegetation growing season.
layers.longDescription: Value of the Plant Phenology Index at the end of the second vegetation growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: The average of the Plant Phenology Index value of the minima on left and right sides of the season.
layers.longDescription: The average of the Plant Phenology Index value of the minima on left and right sides of the first season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: The average of the Plant Phenology Index value of the minima on left and right sides of the season.
layers.longDescription: The average of the Plant Phenology Index value of the minima on left and right sides of the second season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Maximum value that the Plant Phenology Index reaches during the vegetation growing season i.e. value of the Plant Phenology Index at the maximum season date.
layers.longDescription: Maximum value that the Plant Phenology Index reaches during the first vegetation growing season i.e. value of the Plant Phenology Index at the maximum season date of the first growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Maximum value that the Plant Phenology Index reaches during the vegetation growing season i.e. value of the Plant Phenology Index at the maximum season date.
layers.longDescription: Maximum value that the Plant Phenology Index reaches during the second vegetation growing season i.e. value of the Plant Phenology Index at the maximum season date of the second growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Difference between the Season Maximum value and the Season Minimum Value.
layers.longDescription: Difference between the Season Maximum value and the Season Minimum Value for the first growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Difference between the Season Maximum value and the Season Minimum Value.
layers.longDescription: Difference between the Season Maximum value and the Season Minimum Value for the second growing season. Expressed in [m² / m²].

layers.shortDescription: Number of days between the start and the end of the growing season.
layers.longDescription: Number of days between the start and the end of the first growing season.

layers.shortDescription: Number of days between the start and the end of the growing season.
layers.longDescription: Number of days between the start and the end of the second growing season.

layers.layerBrowserName: Slope of the green-up period - Season 1
layers.shortDescription: Value of the increasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during vegetation green-up.
layers.longDescription: Value of the increasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during the first season vegetation green-up . Expressed in [m² / m² /day]

layers.layerBrowserName: Slope of the green-up period - Season 2
layers.shortDescription: Value of the increasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during vegetation green-up.
layers.longDescription: Value of the increasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during the second season vegetation green-up . Expressed in [m² / m² /day].

layers.layerBrowserName: Slope of the green-down period - Season 1
layers.shortDescription: Absolute value of the decreasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during vegetation green-down.
layers.longDescription: Absolute value of the decreasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during the first season vegetation green-down. Expressed in [m² / m² /day].

layers.layerBrowserName: Slope of the green-down period - Season 2
layers.shortDescription: Absolute value of the decreasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during vegetation green-down.
layers.longDescription: Absolute value of the decreasing rate of Plant Phenology Index during the second season vegetation green-down. Expressed in [m² / m² /day].

layers.shortDescription: Indicate the quality of the start, peak and end of the growing season, as well as the overall quality of the three phases, in a form of a certainty level.
layers.longDescription: Indicate the quality of the start, peak and end of the first growing season, as well as the overall quality of the three phases, in a form of a certainty level.

layers.shortDescription: Indicate the quality of the start, peak and end of the growing season, as well as the overall quality of the three phases, in a form of a certainty level.
layers.longDescription: Indicate the quality of the start, peak and end of the second growing season, as well as the overall quality of the three phases, in a form of a certainty level.
